PS-BGID-001.author: admin
PS-BGID-001.text: Reviewer challenge: the intake step does not name the return-path turnaround. Is the SME-supplied "usually same day" formal or aspirational? Worth promoting to a real SLA.

PS-BGID-003.author: admin
PS-BGID-003.text: The step says Legal returns wording 'within SLA' but doesn't name what the SLA is. Real desks have a 2-business-day standard for bespoke wording but Legal often misses it without anyone tracking the breach. Either set the SLA here (2 BD) or link to wherever it's owned — leaving it implicit is exactly why FP-BGID-002 (no queue visibility) exists.

PS-BGID-003.author: Process Specialist
PS-BGID-003.text:
Process Specialist — comment-review summary.

Comment incorporated. The reviewer correctly identified that the sla field was blank and the step body described "no committed turnaround" without naming any convention. Agreed change applied:

— sla field set to: "2 business days (bespoke wording — informal desk convention; not tracked in TFS)"
— "What happens" block updated: the final sentence now reads "Legal review operates against an informal 2-business-day desk convention, though this target is not committed to the client, not tracked in TFS, and carries no escalation trigger when breached."

The wording deliberately distinguishes informal convention from a formally committed SLA, preserving the accuracy of FP-BGID-001 ("no committed turnaround") and FP-BGID-002 ("no committed or communicated SLA") — both friction points remain valid because the 2-BD convention is neither committed nor communicated to clients.

Implicated elements to watch:
— TD-BGID-002 and ASM-BGID-003: the target-state assumption commits Legal to a 5-business-day formal SLA, which is more conservative than the 2-BD informal As-Is convention. Recommend confirming with Legal whether a 5-BD target is intentional (e.g. to accommodate complex cases) or whether the As-Is 2-BD convention should inform a tighter target-state commitment.
— PP-BGID-002: root cause references "no SLA" — still accurate for the As-Is; no edit needed.

PS-BGID-003 remains in-progress pending SME re-approval on the card.

CP-BGID-001.author: admin
CP-BGID-001.text: This control says Trade Finance Manager approves — but in the four guarantee desks I've reviewed across BNP, UniCredit and our own bank, the approval ceiling matters: above EUR 5m (or 2m post-conflict-update) it goes to Head of Trade Finance not the Manager. Does the control document handle that ceiling, or is it relying on TFS routing alone? Audit Finding AF-BGID-002 (BaFin MaRisk gap on four-eyes timestamp) suggests the latter.

II-BGID-001.author: admin
II-BGID-001.text: Linking IR-BGID-002 (EU AI Act high-risk classification) — has anyone checked whether a screener that suggests legal-language changes lands in Annex III of the AI Act? If yes, the timeline assumption in TD-BGID-002 needs human-in-the-loop documentation, conformance assessment, and CE marking before go-live. That's 6+ months on top of build.
